steps.title: Use the vertical-shift shortcut
steps.content: The unknown $n$ is added to every $y$-value, so it only shifts the line up or down. A vertical shift does not change slope. Set $n=0$ to make the table numeric: $(-6,184)$, $(-3,92)$, and $(0,0)$.

steps.title: Read the slope
steps.content: Run $y_1 \sim m x_1+b$ in Desmos. The regression gives $m=-\frac{92}{3}$, which is the same result as $\frac{(n+92)-(n+184)}{-3-(-6)}=\frac{-92}{3}$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: The slope is fixed; it should not still contain $n$. Choice A, $-\frac{92}{3}$, is the only matching choice.
